que: In Compose Preview, what is the primary goal?
options: replace testing entirely
render composables in Android Studio preview
skip state/data modeling
avoid understanding requirements

que: Which API/concept is most central to Compose Preview?
options: manual polling loop for all tasks
legacy XML-only patterns unrelated to this topic
random utility class name
@Preview

que: What is a recommended best practice for Compose Preview?
options: optimize before verifying correctness
mix unrelated responsibilities in one function
ignore edge/error states
iterate UI quickly without deploying each change

que: Which is a common mistake when implementing Compose Preview?
options: follow official docs for API usage
using Preview for runtime navigation logic
write focused tests for behavior
keep boundaries clear

que: For production code, which approach fits Compose Preview best?
options: Use Compose Preview with clear state flow and testable logic
Use Compose Preview while bypassing architecture boundaries
Use Compose Preview and suppress all warnings
Use Compose Preview only as theory with no implementation

que: A teammate asks where to start with Compose Preview. What is best advice?
options: avoid feedback from tests/review
skip running the app after changes
copy an unknown snippet without context
Read official docs (https://developer.android.com/develop/ui/compose/tooling/previews) and implement a small sample

que: Which statement about Compose Preview is most accurate?
options: Compose Preview is most effective when paired with clear ownership of state/data
Compose Preview is mainly about adding boilerplate
Compose Preview should be applied identically in every layer
Compose Preview removes the need for any testing

que: In a code review for Compose Preview, what would you flag?
options: explicit modeling of UI/data states
Code that hides failures instead of handling them explicitly
traceable data flow between layers
small pure functions with clear names

que: Which scenario is the strongest example of using Compose Preview correctly?
options: an implementation with no error states
a slide deck with no runnable code
a large refactor without tests
A small, working feature that demonstrates @Preview in context

que: To learn Compose Preview deeply, what should you do next?
options: Build, test, profile (if needed), and iterate using Compose Preview patterns
memorize definitions only
skip verification to save time
avoid official references
